title: Sodium p-(Mercaptoacetamido)benzenesulphonate as a Volumetric Reagent for Determination of Copper
author: N. S. Poonia
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Sodium  p -(mercaptoacetamido)benzenesulphonate forms an intense blackish blue, water-soluble complex with Cu 2+  below  p H 9.0. The colour changes abruptly to light yellowish green at the Cu : reagent ratio of 1:2 in the  p H range 4.5 to 8.0. The iodine liberated by the action of potassium iodide on Cu 2+  is quanti­tatively titrated against a standard solution of the reagent. Copper has been determined by both of these reactions in presence of a large number of foreign lone. The results have been compared with the iodometric determination of copper with sodium thiosulphate.
steps_raw: Sodium  p -(mercaptoacetamido)benzenesulphonate forms an intense blackish blue, water-soluble complex with Cu 2+  below  p H 9.0. The iodine liberated by the action of potassium iodide on Cu 2+  is quanti­tatively titrated against a standard solution of the reagent. Copper has been determined by both of these reactions in presence of a large number of foreign lone. The results have been compared with the iodometric determination of copper with sodium thiosulphate.
